scope.area: installer, loopback smoke, uninstall, purge, and cleanup timing
coverage: disposable installer clone flow from a local bare snapshot
runtime boot and loopback smoke validation through `Host` headers
purge timing and final residue cleanup

summary.highLevelVerdict: The March 23, 2026 follow-up lifecycle run completed the true installer clone path, scaffolded `www.fakedomain.com`, stopped cleanly, uninstalled cleanly, and purged the preserved data trees. The main blocker did not change: the loopback smoke phase still failed because the manager process crash-looped on `EACCES` while opening `/var/opt/ehecoatl/tenants/fakedomain.com/www/config.json`. Cleanup behavior also still needs work because uninstall left `install-meta.env` behind until purge, and purge still left a stopped root PM2 entry that required one manual `sudo pm2 delete Ehecoatl` step.

commands.snapshotPrep: Create `/tmp/ehecoatl-lifecycle-2026-03-23-125318/source`, commit a disposable snapshot excluding `.git`, `.vscode`, `app/node_modules`, and `app/_log`, then clone it as `/tmp/ehecoatl-lifecycle-2026-03-23-125318/remote.git`.
commands.loopbackSmoke: curl -sk --max-time 5 -H 'Host: fakedomain.com' https://127.0.0.1/htm/index.htm, then route checks for `/`, `/htm/index.htm`, `/htm/cached.htm`, `/hello`, and `/session` with `Host: fakedomain.com` and `Host: www.fakedomain.com`

details: The lifecycle used a disposable source snapshot and a local bare git remote, so `installer.sh` followed the true clone-to-`/opt/ehecoatl` path instead of reusing the authoring checkout.
The authoring checkout remained available for `setup/purge-data.sh` after uninstall removed `/opt/ehecoatl`.
Pre-run checks confirmed no pre-existing `/opt/ehecoatl`, `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, `/srv/opt/ehecoatl`, or root PM2 entries.
The installer completed the true clone flow into `/opt/ehecoatl` and handed off to `setup/setup.sh` successfully.
The setup prompt was answered with `fakedomain.com`, and setup reported that `www.fakedomain.com` was created.
The CLI symlink pointed at `/opt/ehecoatl/cli/ehecoatl`, and runtime data trees were created under `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl`.
`sudo npm run start` returned successfully in 580 ms and PM2 launched two root cluster instances immediately.
No successful loopback response was observed between `2026-03-23T12:54:55+00:00` and `2026-03-23T12:58:17+00:00`.

The captured route checks for both `Host: fakedomain.com` and `Host: www.fakedomain.com` returned `HTTP_CODE=000` with empty headers and empty bodies instead of the expected redirect, HTML, and JSON smoke responses.
A direct stop still completed cleanly in about 2.6 seconds, and a post-stop curl returned exit code `7` with `HTTP_CODE=000`, which matches connection refusal after shutdown.

blocker.symptom: The manager process crash-looped instead of serving the scaffold tenant.
blocker.supportingObservation: The scaffold host directory and `config.json` file were owned by `e_tenant_www.fakedomain.com:e_tenant_www.fakedomain.com` with only `user::rwx`, `group::r-x`, and `other::---` ACL entries. No named ACL entry was present for `e_manager`, which matches the repeated PM2 crash reason.

details: The uninstall script removed `/opt/ehecoatl` and `/usr/local/bin/ehecoatl` successfully.
The expected preserved data trees under `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` remained after uninstall, which matches the documented preservation boundary.
Uninstall logged installation-metadata removal, but `/etc/opt/ehecoatl/install-meta.env` still existed after uninstall until purge removed the whole `/etc/opt/ehecoatl` tree.
After purge, the root PM2 process list still retained a stopped `Ehecoatl` entry even though all filesystem residue was gone.
One manual `sudo pm2 delete Ehecoatl` step was required to finish with an actually clean PM2 state.

requiredChecks.item: A disposable execution target is chosen so uninstall does not destroy the authoring workspace
requiredChecks.note: A disposable snapshot, bare remote, and separate authoring checkout were used.

requiredChecks.item: Installer or setup completes and scaffolds the `tenant-minimal` host for `www.fakedomain.com`
requiredChecks.note: Setup reported successful scaffold creation for `www.fakedomain.com`.

requiredChecks.item: Loopback access proves `GET /`, `GET /htm/index.htm`, `GET /htm/cached.htm`, `GET /hello`, and `GET /session` while sending `Host: fakedomain.com` or `Host: www.fakedomain.com`
requiredChecks.note: No successful loopback response was observed, and the recorded route checks returned `HTTP_CODE=000` because the manager could not read the scaffold host config.

requiredChecks.item: Runtime stop completes without hanging or supervised-child teardown drift
requiredChecks.note: A direct stop completed in 2551 ms and left the PM2 app in stopped state.

requiredChecks.item: Uninstall removes binaries, project files, and install metadata while preserving runtime data
requiredChecks.note: Project files and CLI were removed and runtime data was preserved, but `install-meta.env` remained until purge.

requiredChecks.item: Purge removes the preserved `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` trees
requiredChecks.note: All three preserved data trees were removed.

requiredChecks.item: No residual PM2 process entry remains from the tested run
requiredChecks.note: Not after purge alone. One manual `sudo pm2 delete Ehecoatl` step was required.

requiredChecks.item: A `report/performance` artifact is written for install, smoke, stop, uninstall, and purge timings
requiredChecks.note: This report is the matching follow-up lifecycle artifact.

anomalies: The change from a localhost scaffold domain to `www.fakedomain.com` did not change the main blocker; the manager still crash-looped on host-config ACL access.
The scaffold host directory and `config.json` file did not receive a named ACL entry for `e_manager`.
The recorded loopback route checks for both `fakedomain.com` and `www.fakedomain.com` returned `HTTP_CODE=000` with no headers or body.
`setup/uninstall.sh` logged installation-metadata removal, but `/etc/opt/ehecoatl/install-meta.env` still existed until purge removed `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`.
`purge-data.sh` removed the data trees but did not clear the stopped root PM2 `Ehecoatl` entry.

nextAction.failedPhase: Validate Localhost Smoke Routes And Safe Stop
nextAction.observedSymptom: Manager crash-loop on `www.fakedomain.com` scaffold access with `EACCES` against the host config file
recommendedFixes: Fix scaffold ACL application so the manager runtime user can traverse the tenant host directory and read the host config file before loopback smoke checks begin.
Fix uninstall so its installation-metadata removal log matches reality.
Decide whether uninstall or purge should also clear the root PM2 entry, or document the required manual `pm2 delete` cleanup.
nextAction.followUpDisposition: The installer-loopback-uninstall lifecycle is still not validated for this March 23, 2026 revision. The `fakedomain.com` follow-up confirmed the same ACL blocker and the same cleanup residue behavior under a non-localhost scaffold domain.
